revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。標高182.4m。 == 概要 == 宇都宮市北西部から連なる山...'
revision.text:
'''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。標高182.4m。
== 概要 == 宇都宮市北西部から連なる山系は北から当山を経て[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]と連なり、宇都宮市街地はこの三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系を東西から挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（水道山東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（水道山南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（水道山西麓）が立地する。また水道山の北麓は[[長岡公園]]として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北側の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」があって、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
戸祭山が「水道山」と呼ばれる所以は、当山の西麓にある宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場の水道塔]]が建てられたことによる。現在は建て替えが完了し近代的な装いとなっているが、当初は[[大谷石]]建築の全国的にも珍しい水道塔であった。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない規模の大谷石造りの平和塔（仏舎利塔）がある。
戸祭山の歴史は古く、付近の遺跡としては、瓦を焼いた窯の跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]（推定で[[奈良時代]]の遺跡）などがあり、水道山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
[[Category:栃木県の山|とまつりやま]] [[Category:宇都宮市|とまつりやま]]

'''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。通称、「水道山」。標高182.4m。
== 概要 == 宇都宮市北西部から連なる山系は北から当山を経て[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]と連なり、宇都宮市街地はこの三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系を東西から挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（水道山東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（水道山南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（水道山西麓）が立地する。また水道山の北麓は[[長岡公園]]として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北側の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」があって、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
戸祭山が「水道山」と呼ばれる所以は、当山の西麓にある宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場の水道塔]]が建てられたことによる。現在は建て替えが完了し近代的な装いとなっているが、当初は[[大谷石]]建築の全国的にも珍しい水道塔であった。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない規模の大谷石造りの平和塔（仏舎利塔）がある。
戸祭山の歴史は古く、付近の遺跡としては、瓦を焼いた窯の跡である[[水道山瓦窯跡]]（推定で[[奈良時代]]の遺跡）などがあり、水道山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
[[Category:栃木県の山|とまつりやま]] [[Category:宇都宮市|とまつりやま]]

'''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。通称、「水道山」。標高182.4m。
== 概要 == 宇都宮市北西部から連なる山系は、当山を経て[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]と続き、宇都宮市の中北部市街地はこの三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系の東西には、これを挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
戸祭山は通称「水道山」と呼ばれるが、その所以は、当山西麓に宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場の水道塔]]があることによる。現在は建て替えが完了し近代的な装いとなっているが、当初は[[大谷石]]建築の全国的にも珍しい水道塔であった。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない大きさを有す「平和塔（仏舎利塔）」がある。
戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の遺跡として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (栃木県)|下野薬師寺]]や[[国分寺 (栃木県)|下野国分寺]]の瓦を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。

戸祭山は通称「水道山」と呼ばれるが、その所以は、当山西麓に宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場の水道塔]]があることによる。現在は建て替えが完了し近代的な装いとなっているが、当初は[[大谷石]]建築の全国的にも珍しい水道塔であった。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない大きさを有す「平和塔（仏舎利塔）」がある。
== 歴史 == 戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の遺跡として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (下野市)|下野薬師寺]]や[[国分寺 (栃木県)|下野国分寺]]の瓦を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
== 周辺環境 == 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
[[Category:栃木県の山|とまつりやま]] [[Category:宇都宮市|とまつりやま]]

'''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。通称、「'''水道山'''」。標高182.4m。
== 地理 == 宇都宮市北西部から連なる山系は、当山を経て[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]と続き、宇都宮市の中北部市街地はこの三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系の東西には、これを挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山は通称「水道山」と呼ばれるが、その所以は、当山西麓に宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場配水池|戸祭配水場水道塔]]があることによる。現在の新築された近代的な装いの水道塔も人目を引くが、当初の建物は[[煉瓦]]と[[大谷石]]の複合建築という全国的にも珍しい貴重な水道塔であった。[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）[[10月18日]]には[[戸祭配水場配水池]]が国の有形文化財（土地構造物部門・建造物）に登録されている。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない大きさを有す「平和塔（仏舎利塔）」がある。
== 歴史 == 戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の遺跡として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (下野市)|下野薬師寺]]や[[国分寺 (栃木県)|下野国分寺]]の瓦を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
== 周辺環境 == 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。

'''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。通称、「'''水道山'''」。標高182.4m。
== 地理 == 宇都宮市北西部[[羽黒山 (栃木県)|羽黒山]]周辺より連なる[[宇都宮丘陵]]は、当山を経て[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]、[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]と続き、宇都宮市の中北部市街地はこの三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系の東西には、これを挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山は通称「水道山」と呼ばれるが、その所以は、当山西麓に宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場配水池|戸祭配水場水道塔]]があることによる。現在の新築された近代的な装いの水道塔も人目を引くが、当初の建物は[[煉瓦]]と[[大谷石]]の複合建築という全国的にも珍しい貴重な水道塔であった。[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）[[10月18日]]には[[戸祭配水場配水池]]が国の有形文化財（土地構造物部門・建造物）に登録されている。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない大きさを有す「平和塔（仏舎利塔）」がある。
== 歴史 == 戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の遺跡として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[戸祭大塚古墳|大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (下野市)|下野薬師寺]]や[[国分寺 (栃木県)|下野国分寺]]の瓦を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
== 周辺環境 == 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりやま}} [[Category:栃木県の山]] [[Category:栃木県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]

== 歴史 == 戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の[[遺跡]]として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[戸祭大塚古墳|大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (下野市)|下野薬師寺]]や[[上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|河内郡衙]]の[[瓦]]を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
== 周辺環境 == 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
また、平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりやま}} [[Category:栃木県の山]] [[Category:栃木県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:宇都宮市]]

{{otheruses|[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]の'''戸祭山'''|同市東戸祭一丁目にある'''戸祭山祥雲寺'''|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}} '''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。通称、「'''水道山'''」。標高186.3m。
== 地理 == [[栃木県]]北西部山系は[[日光二荒山神社]]や[[日光東照宮]]を祀る[[日光山|日光山内]]から[[小倉山 (日光市)|小倉山]]、[[愛宕山 (日光市)|愛宕山]]、[[茶臼山 (日光市)|茶臼山]]、[[浅間山 (日光市)|浅間山]]と連なり、宇都宮市北部の[[篠井富屋連峰]]を経て当山、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]と続き、そしてその最南端部に[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]が位置する。当山はまた[[宇都宮丘陵]]の一部を成している。宇都宮市街地は、当山、八幡山、明神山の三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系の東西には、これを挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山は通称「水道山」と呼ばれるが、その所以は、当山西麓に宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場配水池|戸祭配水場水道塔]]があることによる。現在の新築された近代的な装いの水道塔も人目を引くが、当初の建物は[[煉瓦]]と[[大谷石]]の複合建築という全国的にも珍しい貴重な水道塔であった。[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）[[10月18日]]には[[戸祭配水場配水池]]が国の[[登録有形文化財]]（土木構造物・生活関連）に登録されている。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない大きさを有す「平和塔（仏舎利塔）」がある。
== 歴史 == 戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の[[遺跡]]として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[戸祭大塚古墳|大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (下野市)|下野薬師寺]]や[[上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|河内郡衙]]の[[瓦]]を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
== 周辺環境 == 平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。 ===宇都宮の北の文教地区=== 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見ヶ丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「[[戸祭グリーンヒル]]」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[うつのみや文化の森]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にある[[宇都宮美術館]]や[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりやま}} [[Category:栃木県の山]] [[Category:栃木県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]]

{{otheruses|[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]の'''戸祭山'''|同市東戸祭一丁目にある'''戸祭山祥雲寺'''|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}} [[ファイル:Mt. == 周辺環境 == 平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。 ===宇都宮の北の文教地区=== 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見が丘|富士見が丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭|戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「戸祭グリーンヒル」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[宇都宮美術館|うつのみや文化の森（宇都宮美術館）]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にあるや[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりやま}} [[Category:アジアの山]] [[Category:栃木県の山]] [[Category:栃木県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]の'''戸祭山'''|同市東戸祭一丁目にある'''戸祭山祥雲寺'''|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}} [[ファイル:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|水道山の赤レンガ階段]] [[ファイル:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|戸祭配水場]] [[ファイル:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|栃木県平和仏舎利塔]] '''戸祭山'''（とまつりやま）は、[[栃木県]][[宇都宮市]]にある[[山]]。通称、「'''水道山'''」。標高186.3m。
== 地理 == [[栃木県]]北西部山系は[[日光二荒山神社]]や[[日光東照宮]]を祀る[[日光山|日光山内]]から[[小倉山 (日光市)|小倉山]]、[[愛宕山 (日光市)|愛宕山]]、[[茶臼山 (日光市)|茶臼山]]、[[浅間山 (日光市)|浅間山]]と連なり、宇都宮市北部の[[篠井富屋連峰]]を経て当山、[[八幡山 (宇都宮市)|八幡山]]と続き、そしてその最南端部に[[明神山 (宇都宮市)|明神山]]が位置する。当山はまた[[宇都宮丘陵]]の一部を成している。宇都宮市街地は、当山、八幡山、明神山の三峰を中心としてその山麓の平坦部に形成されている。また、この一連の山系の東西には、これを挟むように東に[[田川 (利根川水系)|田川]]、西に[[釜川]]が流れる。
戸祭山は通称「水道山」と呼ばれるが、その所以は、当山西麓に宇都宮市上下水道局・[[戸祭配水場配水池|戸祭配水場水道塔]]があることによる。現在の新築された近代的な装いの水道塔も人目を引くが、当初の建物は[[煉瓦]]と[[大谷石]]の複合建築という全国的にも珍しい貴重な水道塔であった。[[2006年]]（[[平成]]18年）[[10月18日]]には[[戸祭配水場配水池]]が国の[[登録有形文化財]]（土木構造物・生活関連）に登録されている。また、山頂付近にはやはり大谷石建築で、他に例を見ない大きさを有す「平和塔（仏舎利塔）」がある。
== 歴史 == 戸祭山周辺の歴史は古く、付近の[[遺跡]]として、[[古墳時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[古墳]]とされる[[戸祭大塚古墳|大塚古墳]]・[[谷口山古墳]]・[[瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|瓦塚古墳]]・[[長岡百穴古墳]]、また奈良時代に[[薬師寺 (下野市)|下野薬師寺]]や[[上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|河内郡衙]]の[[瓦]]を焼いた窯の跡とされる[[水道山瓦窯跡]]などがあり、戸祭山北部の一連の遺跡群を結ぶ「[[豊郷まほろばの道]]」が整備されている。
== 周辺環境 == 平坦な[[関東平野]]の中にあって、戸祭山は市街周辺では有数の見晴らしのよい高台であり、冬の晴れた朝には南東方向に[[富士山]]が遠望できる。 ===宇都宮の北の文教地区=== 戸祭山の周辺には、宇都宮市有数の大規模住宅団地である「[[富士見が丘|富士見が丘団地]]」（東麓）、「[[戸祭|戸祭台団地]]」（南麓）および「戸祭グリーンヒル」（西麓）が立地する。また、この北麓は「[[長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|長岡公園]]」として整備されており、その北側を走る[[宇都宮環状道路]]のさらに北の丘陵地には、[[宇都宮美術館|うつのみや文化の森（宇都宮美術館）]]を中心とする「[[豊郷台]]団地」となっており、付近にあるや[[帝京大学]]宇都宮キャンパスと相まって、宇都宮の北の文教地区を形成している。
== アクセス == ;路線バス * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[宇都宮駅]]西口から ** [[関東自動車 (栃木県)|関東バス]] 清住経由細谷車庫行き、[[日光東照宮]]行き、山王団地行きなど乗車15分、「中戸祭」下車徒歩10分（戸祭配水場）または20分（平和塔） ** 関東バス 西塙田・戸祭台循環線乗車15分、「第二グリーンヒル南」下車徒歩5分（戸祭配水場）または「第一グリーンヒル」下車徒歩5分（平和塔・戸祭配水場） 中戸祭から歩くと、直線道路を真っ直ぐ歩いて[[釜川]]を渡って旧[[戸祭田圃]]の先に真っ直ぐに伸びる赤レンガの階段があり、その頂上に戸祭配水場水道塔が聳える。
{{DEFAULTSORT:とまつりやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:栃木県の山]] [[Category:栃木県の自然景勝地]] [[Category:宇都宮市の地理]]

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' (戸祭山), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' (戸祭山祥雲寺) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|[[Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)]]}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|Red Brick Steps of Suido-yama]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' (戸祭山) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''Suido-yama'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the [[Nikkō Sannai]], which enshrines [[Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] and [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], through [[Ogura-yama (Nikkō)]], [[Atago-yama (Nikkō)]], [[Chausu-yama (Nikkō)]], and [[Asama-yama (Nikkō)]], connecting to the [[Shinoi-Tomiya Range]] in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to [[Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)]], and ends at its southernmost peak, [[Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)]]. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the [[Utsunomiya Hills]]. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the [[Tagawa River]] flows to the east and the [[Kamagawa River]] to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "Suido-yama" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s [[Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility]] located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and [[Ōya stone]]. On October 18, 2006, the [[Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility]] was registered as a [[Registered Tangible Cultural Property]] (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "Peace Stupa" (仏舎利塔) made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as [[Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]], [[Taniguchi-yama Kofun]], [[Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)]], and [[Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun]], which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the [[Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins]], where tiles for temples like [[Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji]] and government offices like the [[Kawachi District Office Ruins]] were fired during the Nara period. The "Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat [[Kantō Plain]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, [[Mount Fuji]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "Fujimigaoka Danchi" (east slope), "Tomatsuri-dai Danchi" (south slope), and "Tomatsuri Green Hill" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as [[Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)]], and further north beyond the [[Utsunomiya Ring Road]] is the "Toyosato-dai Danchi," centered around the [[Utsunomiya Museum of Art]] within the "Utsunomiya Cultural Forest." Alongside the [[Teikyo University]] Utsunomiya Campus, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From [[JR Utsunomiya Station]] West Exit: ** [[Kantō Bus]] via Kiyozumi to Hosoya Garage, [[Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], or Sannō Danchi: 15 minutes, get off at "Naka-Tomatsuri," then a 10-minute walk to the [[Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility]] or a 20-minute walk to the Peace Stupa. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "Dai-ni Green Hill Minami" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "Dai-ichi Green Hill" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the [[Kamagawa River]], with red brick steps extending beyond the old [[Tomatsuri Rice Fields]] towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

[[Category:戸祭山]][[ja:戸祭山]]

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

#REDIRECT [[Tomatsuri-yama]]

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|12=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|12=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|12=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
[[Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun]] ...ills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}. {{draft categories| [[Category:List of Mountains]] [[Category:Mountains of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Scenic Spots of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Utsunomiya]] [[Category:戸祭山]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:戸祭山]] [[sn:Tomatsuri-yama]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tomatsuri-yama}}

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|12=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|12=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|12=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|12=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|12=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|12=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|12=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|12=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|12=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=draft|12=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft|12=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft|12=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|12=draft|12=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=draft|12=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft|12=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|12=draft|12=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|12=draft|12=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft|12=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft|12=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft|12=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft|12=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|12=draft|12=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=draft|12=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=draft|12=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft|12=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft|12=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|12=draft|12=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|12=draft|12=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=draft|12=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=draft|12=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft|12=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=draft|12=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|12=draft|12=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=draft|12=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=draft|12=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft|12=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=draft|12=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=draft|12=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=draft|12=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft|12=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=draft|12=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=draft|12=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft|12=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=draft|12=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
[[Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun]] ...ills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|12=draft|12=draft}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=draft|12=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft|12=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=draft|12=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft|12=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|12=draft|12=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=draft|12=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=draft|12=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=draft|12=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=draft|12=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=draft|12=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|12=draft|12=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|12=draft|12=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=draft|12=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=draft|12=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=draft|12=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=draft|12=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|12=draft|12=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=draft|12=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=draft|12=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|12=draft|12=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=draft|12=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=draft|12=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=draft|12=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=draft|12=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=draft|12=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=draft|12=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=draft|12=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=draft|12=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=draft|12=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
