{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

[[ja:戸祭山]] [[sn:Tomatsuri-yama]]
{{citation needed|Tomatsuri-yama}} {{translated page|ja|戸祭山|version=99543508|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.

revision.text:
{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
[[Nagaoka Hyakuana Kofun]] ...ills of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site is located on a plateau flanked by the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone ... 6 KB (721 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 [[Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun]] ...omiya City Board|1992|p=55|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on the north side of {{ill|Tomatsuri-yama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and west of the {{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. N ... 6 KB (758 words) - 06:00, 19 January 2025 Taniguchi-yama Kofun ...{{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. N ... 6 KB (758 words) - 06:00, 19 January 2025 * [[Taniguchi-yama Kofun]] ...{{ill|Tagawa River (Tone River System)|ja|田川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near {{ill|Tomatsuriyama|ja|'''戸祭山'''|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. {{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.

== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

[[sn:Tomatsuri-yama]] [[ja:戸祭山]]

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.

There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|qq=}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|qq=}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi District Office Ruins|qq=}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|qq=}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Plain|qq=}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|qq=}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|qq=}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|qq=}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Museum of Art|qq=}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|qq=}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|qq=}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|qq=}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|qq=}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|qq=}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|qq=}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

[[sn:Tomatsuri-yama]] [[ja:戸祭山]]

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|qq=}}, which enshrines {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and {{ill|Ōya stone|ja|大谷石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōya stone|qq=}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|qq=}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|qq=}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kawachi District Office Ruins|ja|上神主・茂原官衙遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi District Office Ruins|qq=}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|qq=}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|ja|関東平野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Plain|qq=}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|ja|富士山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Fuji|qq=}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|qq=}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|qq=}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|ja|宇都宮美術館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Museum of Art|qq=}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|qq=}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|qq=}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|JR Utsunomiya Station|ja|宇都宮駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Utsunomiya Station|qq=}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|qq=}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|ja|日光東照宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Tōshō-gū|qq=}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|qq=}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|qq=}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|qq=}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|qq=}}, which enshrines [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] and [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya stone]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|qq=}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|qq=}} and government offices like the [[:en:Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|Kawachi District Office Ruins]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|qq=}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|qq=}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|qq=}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the [[:en:Utsunomiya Museum of Art|Utsunomiya Museum of Art]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|qq=}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|qq=}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|JR Utsunomiya Station]] West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|qq=}}, [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|qq=}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|qq=}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|qq=}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a [[mountain]] in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]]|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|qq=}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a [[mountain]] located in [[Utsunomiya]], [[Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|qq=}}, which enshrines [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] and [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|qq=}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of [[brick]] and [[:en:Ōya stone|Ōya stone]]. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the [[Nara period]]. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|qq=}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|qq=}} and government offices like the [[:en:Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|Kawachi District Office Ruins]] were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|qq=}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]], offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, [[:en:Mount Fuji|Mount Fuji]] can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|qq=}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|qq=}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the [[:en:Utsunomiya Museum of Art|Utsunomiya Museum of Art]] within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|qq=}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|qq=}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From [[:en:Utsunomiya Station|JR Utsunomiya Station]] West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|qq=}}, [[:en:Nikkō Tōshō-gū|Nikkō Tōshō-gū]], or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|qq=}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|qq=}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|qq=}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|qq=}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|qq=}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
