<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:戸祭山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:戸祭山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:戸祭山]]
{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日光の社寺}}, which enshrines {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of {{ill|Brick|en|Brick|WD=Q40089|lt=Brick|1=Brick}} and {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya stone|1=Ōya stone}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野薬師寺跡}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|en|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|WD=Q101847935|lt=Kawachi District Office Ruins|1=Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|WD=Q11449808|lt=Utsunomiya Museum of Art|1=Utsunomiya Museum of Art}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=JR Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日光の社寺}}, which enshrines {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of {{ill|Brick|en|Brick|WD=Q40089|lt=Brick|1=Brick}} and {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya stone|1=Ōya stone}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野薬師寺跡}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|en|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|WD=Q101847935|lt=Kawachi District Office Ruins|1=Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|WD=Q11449808|lt=Utsunomiya Museum of Art|1=Utsunomiya Museum of Art}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=JR Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|WD=Q11449808|lt=Utsunomiya Museum of Art|1=Utsunomiya Museum of Art}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=JR Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:戸祭山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:戸祭山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:戸祭山]]
{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日光の社寺}}, which enshrines {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of {{ill|Brick|en|Brick|WD=Q40089|lt=Brick|1=Brick}} and {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya stone|1=Ōya stone}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野薬師寺跡}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|en|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|WD=Q101847935|lt=Kawachi District Office Ruins|1=Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|WD=Q11449808|lt=Utsunomiya Museum of Art|1=Utsunomiya Museum of Art}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=JR Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.

revision.text:
{{otheruses|'''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}), a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}|'''Tomatsuri-yama Shōun-ji''' ({{ill|戸祭山祥雲寺|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山祥雲寺|qq=}}) located in Higashi-Tomatsuri 1-chome, Utsunomiya|{{ill|Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|ja|祥雲寺 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōun-ji (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}}} [[File:Mt. Suido Brick Steps.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}} Red Brick Steps]] [[File:Tomatsuri Reservoir.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] [[File:Tochigi Prefecture Peace Chorten.jpg|thumb|{{ill|Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja|栃木県平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}]] '''Tomatsuri-yama''' ({{ill|戸祭山|ja|戸祭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/戸祭山|qq=}}) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=Mountain|1=Mountain}} located in {{ill|Utsunomiya|en|Utsunomiya|WD=Q200589|lt=Utsunomiya|1=Utsunomiya}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is also commonly known as "'''{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}'''." The mountain has an elevation of 186.3 meters.
== Geography == The northwestern mountain range of Tochigi Prefecture stretches from the {{ill|Nikkō Sannai|ja|日光山内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Sannai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日光の社寺}}, which enshrines {{ill|Futarasan shrine|en|Futarasan shrine|WD=Q701927|lt=Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|1=Futarasan shrine}} and {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, through {{ill|Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja|小倉山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogura-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja|愛宕山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja|茶臼山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chausu-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja|浅間山 (日光市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asama-yama (Nikkō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, connecting to the {{ill|Shinoi-Tomiya Range|ja|篠井富屋連峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinoi-Tomiya Range|qq=}} in northern Utsunomiya. It continues to this mountain, then to {{ill|Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|八幡山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}, and ends at its southernmost peak, {{ill|Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|ja|明神山 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myojin-yama (Utsunomiya)|qq=}}. Tomatsuri-yama is part of the {{ill|Utsunomiya Hills|ja|宇都宮丘陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Hills|qq=}}. The urban area of Utsunomiya developed around the foothills of Tomatsuri-yama, Hachiman-yama, and Myojin-yama. Additionally, the {{ill|Tagawa River|ja|田川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagawa River|qq=}} flows to the east and the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}} to the west of this mountain range.
Tomatsuri-yama is commonly referred to as "{{ill|Suido-yama|ja|水道山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama|qq=}}" because of the [[Utsunomiya City Waterworks Bureau]]'s {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located on its western slope. The current modern water tower is eye-catching, but the original structure was a rare and valuable building made of a combination of {{ill|Brick|en|Brick|WD=Q40089|lt=Brick|1=Brick}} and {{ill|Ōya stone|en|Ōya stone|WD=Q8081802|lt=Ōya stone|1=Ōya stone}}. On October 18, 2006, the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場配水池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|qq=}} was registered as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} (civil engineering structure, life-related). Near the summit, there is also a large "{{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" made of Ōya stone, unique in its size.
== History == The history of the Tomatsuri-yama area is ancient, with archaeological sites such as {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Yaguchiyama Kofun|lt=Taniguchi-yama Kofun|ja|谷口山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taniguchi-yama Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja|瓦塚古墳 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawarazuka Kofun (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|ja|長岡百穴古墳|1=Nagaoka Hundred Caves|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hyakketsu Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Hundred Caves|qq=}}, which date from the [[Kofun period]] to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. There are also remains of kilns, such as the {{ill|Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja|水道山瓦窯跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suido-yama Tile Kiln Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where tiles for temples like {{ill|Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja|薬師寺 (下野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Yakushi-ji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野薬師寺跡}} and government offices like the {{ill|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|en|Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins|WD=Q101847935|lt=Kawachi District Office Ruins|1=Kamikōnushi-Mobara Kanga ruins}} were fired during the Nara period. The "{{ill|Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja|豊郷まほろばの道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato Mahoroba-no-Michi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" walking trail connects these northern Tomatsuri-yama historical sites.
== Surrounding Area == Tomatsuri-yama, being one of the most prominent hills in the flat {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}, offers excellent views. On clear winter mornings, {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|WD=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}} can be seen in the southeast direction.
=== Northern Educational District of Utsunomiya === The area around Tomatsuri-yama includes large residential neighborhoods such as "{{ill|Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja|富士見が丘 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimigaoka Danchi (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (east slope), "{{ill|Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja|戸祭台団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri-dai Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (south slope), and "{{ill|Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja|戸祭グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" (west slope). The northern slope is developed as {{ill|Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja|長岡公園 (宇都宮市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka Park (Utsunomiya)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and further north beyond the {{ill|Utsunomiya Ring Road|ja|宇都宮環状道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Ring Road|qq=}} is the "{{ill|Toyosato-dai Danchi|ja|豊郷台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosato-dai Danchi|qq=}}," centered around the {{ill|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|en|Utsunomiya Museum of Art|WD=Q11449808|lt=Utsunomiya Museum of Art|1=Utsunomiya Museum of Art}} within the "{{ill|Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja|うつのみや文化の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Cultural Forest|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Alongside the {{ill|Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja|帝京大学 宇都宮キャンパス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teikyo University Utsunomiya Campus|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, this area forms the northern educational district of Utsunomiya.
== Access == ; By Bus * From {{ill|Utsunomiya Station|en|Utsunomiya Station|WD=Q801549|lt=JR Utsunomiya Station|1=Utsunomiya Station}} West Exit: ** {{ill|Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|ja|関東自動車 (栃木県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kantō Bus (Tochigi Prefecture)|qq=}} via Kiyozumi to {{ill|Hosoya Garage|ja|細谷車庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosoya Garage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|en|Nikkō Tōshō-gū|WD=Q696641|lt=Nikkō Tōshō-gū|1=Nikkō Tōshō-gū}}, or {{ill|Sannō Danchi|ja|山王団地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannō Danchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Naka-Tomatsuri|ja|中戸祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka-Tomatsuri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 戸祭}}," then a 10-minute walk to the {{ill|Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja|戸祭配水場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Water Distribution Facility|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or a 20-minute walk to the {{ill|Peace Stupa|ja|平和仏舎利塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peace Stupa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Kantō Bus Nishi-Hanada & Tomatsuri-dai Loop Line: 15 minutes, get off at "{{ill|Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja|第二グリーンヒル南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ni Green Hill Minami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Water Distribution Facility or at "{{ill|Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja|第一グリーンヒル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-ichi Green Hill|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" for a 5-minute walk to the Peace Stupa and Water Distribution Facility.
Walking from Naka-Tomatsuri, you'll find a straight road leading over the {{ill|Kamagawa River|ja|釜川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamagawa River|qq=}}, with red brick steps extending beyond the old {{ill|Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja|戸祭田圃|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomatsuri Rice Fields|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} towards the summit where the Tomatsuri Water Distribution Tower stands tall.
